qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: fromJS exhausts the call stack on deeply nested input
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is about prototype pollution from `__proto__` handling in `mergeDeep()`, `mergeDeepWith()`, `merge()`, and `Map.toJS()/toObject()`, while this finding is an unrelated unbounded-recursion DoS in `src/fromJS.js`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: NaN bounds in Range cause non-terminating iteration and process aborts
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory targets prototype pollution in object-materialization and merge APIs when `__proto__` is copied, while this finding is a separate `Range()` NaN-validation failure that leads to crashes or hangs during sequence materialization.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's prototype-pollution root cause in `mergeDeep()`, `mergeDeepWith()`, `merge()`, `Map.toJS()`, or `Map.toObject()`. The only advisory-aligned session result is `plain-object-helper-prototype-poisoning`, but it was finalized as `non_qualifying_vuln`.
evidence: The judge input manifest lists exactly two final `qualifying_vuln` findings: `fromjs-deep-recursion-stack-exhaustion` and `range-nan-iteration-dos`.

Those qualifying findings describe availability bugs in `src/fromJS.js` and `src/Range.js`, not `__proto__`-driven prototype pollution in the merge or `Map.toJS()/toObject()` code paths named by the advisory.

Session artifacts and the component checklist show the prototype-poisoning issue was captured separately as `plain-object-helper-prototype-poisoning`, and that finding remained `non_qualifying_vuln` because repository-owned impact stopped at returned-object prototype mutation.
